result:
1. Rate-limit snippet never throttles — `const [count] = await multi.exec()` (Section 4) destructures ioredis's array of `[err,res]` tuples; `count` is `[null, <n>]`, so `count > limit` compares array to number (always false). Impact: limiter is a no-op on every route. Fix: `const res = await multi.exec(); const count = res[0][1];` (and handle `res === null` for watch-abort). Then regression-test with a 429-forcing integration test.

2. Region prefix missing from key format — Section 4 documents `rl:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}`, but the 2025-12 SEV2 RCA (Section 10) says region prefix was added. Handbook and code are out of sync. Impact: either the doc is stale (users will re-introduce the 2025-12 bug from copy-paste) or the fix never landed. Fix: make key `rl:{region}:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` and update both places; add lint/test asserting key starts with region.

3. `header_sanitize` allowlist does not strip `X-Internal-*` — Section 2 rule 1 lists `X-User-Id`, `X-Org-Id`, `X-Forwarded-For`, `X-Real-IP`, `X-Original-Authorization`, but Section 5 introduces `X-Internal-User-Id`, `X-Internal-Org-Id`, `X-Internal-Roles`, `X-Internal-Auth-Method` as trusted downstream. If a client sets `X-Internal-User-Id: victim` and `header_sanitize` doesn't strip the `X-Internal-*` prefix, auth-bypass to any user. Fix: strip by prefix `X-Internal-` unconditionally on ingress; add a spoofed-header test covering each of the four.

4. "Sliding window" is actually fixed-bucket — Section 4 keys by `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)`. At bucket boundary a client can burst `2 * limit` in a few seconds (end-of-bucket + start-of-next). Impact: documented limits understate real allowance ~2×; attackers at the bucket edge evade. Fix: either rename to "fixed-window" honestly, or implement true sliding window (token bucket in Lua, or `ZADD` of timestamps + `ZCOUNT` over 60s).

5. Rate limiter runs before auth (pipeline step 3 vs step 5) with IP-only keying — no per-authenticated-user tier. Impact: a single authenticated abuser rotating IPs (mobile carrier NAT, Tor, residential proxy pool) bypasses limits; shared-NAT legitimate users get collectively throttled. Fix: keep IP pre-auth limit, add post-auth per-`sub` limit as a second middleware after `auth_jwt`; document the two-tier design in Section 4.

6. 30-second `exp` skew + JWKS stale-while-revalidate up to ~16 min = long revocation window — Section 5 accepts tokens until `exp < Date.now() - 30000`, and Section 2 rule 4 caches JWKS with 16-min soft TTL SWR. No `jti` dedup at edge (Section 5 explicitly defers to downstream). Impact: a compromised or rotated-away key signs tokens accepted for up to ~16 min; individual tokens replayable up to 30s past expiry. Fix: reduce skew to 5s, drop JWKS SWR to hard TTL on any `kid` cache miss, and publish a revocation channel (`auth-svc` → edge pub/sub) that invalidates JWKS + per-`kid` tokens immediately.

7. No `kid` pinning / algorithm-per-key binding described — Section 5 allowlists `RS256, ES256` globally. If JWKS serves both RSA and EC keys, failing to check that the selected JWK's `alg`/`kty` matches the token header's `alg` opens alg/key confusion (e.g., verifying an RSA-signed token against an EC key as if it were a different algorithm in weaker libs). Fix: require the JWT header `kid` to resolve exactly one JWK, and assert `jwk.alg === header.alg` before verify; reject otherwise.

8. 429 response lacks standard `Retry-After` header; uses non-standard body key `retry_after` — Section 4 snippet sets only a JSON field. Clients and CDN/edge caches use the `Retry-After` HTTP header (RFC 7231/6585). Impact: SDK/browser backoff logic ignores your hint; thundering herd on limit recovery. Fix: `res.set('Retry-After', String(secondsUntilNextBucket())).status(429).json(...)` and keep `retry_after` in body for compatibility.

9. 30-second API key LRU (Section 5) = 30-second revocation lag — a partner key leaked and rotated in auth-svc still authenticates at edge for up to 30s across all pods. Fix: on rotation/revoke, `auth-svc` publishes to a Redis pub/sub channel; each edge subscribes and deletes the key from its LRU on message. Fall back to the 30s TTL as belt-and-braces.

10. `trustedClientIp` depends on static `config/trusted_proxies.json` — CloudFront/Cloudflare egress ranges change weekly. Stale file either (a) over-trusts (old decommissioned range now owned by someone else — XFF spoof returns) or (b) under-trusts (legitimate proxied clients get wrong IP keying). Fix: fetch ranges from vendor JSON (CloudFront `ip-ranges.json`, Cloudflare `/ips-v4`) on a 1h timer with a signed/pinned mirror fallback; alert on fetch failures > 24h. Document the refresh path in Section 2 rule 2.
